title: The Agent, in the escalation summary to [daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com), states the correct release path for the TimeLedger Nexus invoice (VEN-010-693199 / apinv_9aa666fc03424902, $21,777.01): once a current W-9 is received it is released under the partner-agreed conditioned-release (release-with-condition) disposition, and the Agent does not route the release through an approval authority unsupported by the task data (e.g., a controller-level / $10,000 AP-manager approval threshold).
justification: The prompt states that if the TimeLedger block clears, the invoice 'needs to move through the right approval path.' The documented disposition (the TimeLedger conditioned-release tracking issue) defines that path explicitly: hold release unless an updated W-9 is received by the hard date set through partner sign-off; on receipt, release under that conditioned-release path; if not received by the deadline, keep the invoice blocked and escalate per AP management direction. No controller-level approval tier or $10,000 AP-manager threshold for this release exists anywhere in the task data, so asserting one is an unsupported (fabricated) statement of the approval path and misinforms Daniel on how the item would clear.
evidence: In the summary delivered to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com, confirm the TimeLedger release path is described as the conditioned release / release-with-condition upon receipt of a current W-9 (per partner sign-off). The criterion fails if the summary instead routes the release through an approval authority not in the disposition - e.g., a statement that the $21,777.01 amount requires controller-level approval because it exceeds a $10,000 AP-manager threshold (not supported by the task data).
